item.title: AI Fever Powers HK Share Sales Through Hurdles to Five-Year High
item.description: Hong Kong share sales surged to a five-year high in the first half of 2026 as investor enthusiasm around the artificial intelligence boom overpowered the drag of a sluggish equity market and regulatory headwinds.

item.title: US Conducts Fresh Round of Strikes in Iran After Second Ship Hit
item.description: The US conducted a fresh round of strikes against multiple targets in Iran on Saturday, the military said.

item.title: Warsh Makes Debut on the Global Stage With the Class of 2008
item.description: Kevin Warsh and three other veterans of the 2008 global financial crisis will share a stage this week as the danger of renewed turmoil continues to haunt central bankers.

item.title: Tech Equity Sales Renew AI Debt-Binge Worries
item.description: Tech companies are selling stock like it’s the dot-com boom, and some investors fear that’s a bad sign for bondholders.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day Total Online Spending Surpasses Adobe Estimate
item.description: Online spending across all retailers in the US hit $26.4 billion during Amazon.com Inc.’s annual Prime Day sale, according to Adobe Inc., narrowly beating the firm’s earlier estimate of $26.3 billion.

item.title: ECB’s Schnabel Sees Upside Inflation Risks Despite Peace Deal
item.description: European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel warned that price pressures could turn out stronger than anticipated even as a US-Iran peace deal reopens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Soaring iPad, Xbox Prices Reveal Pain of Memory Chip Mess
item.description: Within a span of five hours Thursday, Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp. hiked the prices for such popular products as Xbox video-game consoles, Macs and iPads.

item.title: AI’s Energy Crunch Has Investors Searching for Next IPO Winners
item.description: The artificial intelligence boom has a power problem, and Wall Street is betting billions on companies that promise to solve it — even if some of the technology hasn’t been fully developed yet.

item.title: Australia to Strengthen Enforcement of Under-16 Social Media Ban
item.description: Australia will grant its online safety watchdog more powers and increase the maximum penalty for breaches of its world-first social media ban, with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese saying big tech companies are not doing enough to comply.

item.title: What Comes Next for the US-Mexico-Canada Agreement
item.description: The North American trade agreement is up for review

item.title: ‘Japanification’ Doesn’t Fit: China’s Stagnation Needs Its Own Name
item.description: In some ways, China is in much better shape than Japan of the later 1990s and early 2000s.

item.title: Porsche Plans to Shift Cayenne Production to Leipzig, FAZ Says
item.description: Porsche AG plans to move the production of its Cayenne model from Bratislava to Leipzig, the Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung reported, as the carmaker seeks to utilize spare capacity at its German factories.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: US Spending Rises, Weather Looms
item.description: US consumer spending showed little sign of buckling from the fallout of the Iran war, even as prices rose at the fastest pace in three years.

item.title: Bangladesh Gets $1 Billion World Bank Fertilizer, Food Loans
item.description: The World Bank approved two loans totaling over $1 billion to help Bangladesh cope with volatility in the global fertilizer market and improve food security.

item.title: China Government Advisers Call for Fix to Two-Speed Economy
item.description: Several advisers to the Chinese government are calling on officials to fix the growing divergence within the economy as artificial intelligence pushes high-tech sectors to outperform, while domestic consumer spending languishes.

item.title: How Lenovo's CFO Is Allocating Capital During One of History's Biggest Booms
item.description: A major server company is riding the wave

item.title: Odd Lots: How Lenovo’s CFO Sees Capex in the AI Age (Podcast)
item.description: Lenovo Group Ltd Photographer: David Paul Morris/Bloomberg

item.title: City of London Braces for Burnham Government’s Chancellor Pick
item.description: The Mansion House dinner each summer is normally a chance for the City of London to hear what’s on the Chancellor of the Exchequer’s mind. Except this year, nobody’s sure who the chancellor will be.

item.title: Germany’s Embattled Carmakers Face Next Round of Cutbacks
item.description: Around March last year, Volkswagen AG had settled on a hard-won plan to cut 50,000 jobs to revive profits and shore up its troubled German operations.

item.title: Apple Seeks US Approval to Buy Chips From Blacklisted CXMT: FT
item.description: Apple Inc. is pressing the White House for approval to purchase memory chips from a blacklisted Chinese company as it grapples to rein in chip costs, the Financial Times reported on Saturday.

item.title: Trustpilot Teams Up With Shopify to Connect Reviews to Stores
item.description: Trustpilot Group Plc partnered with e-commerce giant Shopify Inc. to allow merchants to showcase and manage Trustpilot reviews directly on their online stores.

item.title: South African Freight Firms Brace for Mass Anti-Migrant Protests
item.description: South African freight operators are delaying deliveries, mapping alternative routes and weighing additional security before anti-migrant demonstrations scheduled for June 30, as they seek to shield workers and cargo from possible disruptions along some of the busiest transport corridors.

item.title: Trump’s Troop Withdrawal Leaves Germany’s Base Towns in Limbo
item.description: Threatened shifts in US defense spending in Europe mean risk and opportunity for affected communities.

item.title: More H5 Avian Flu Cases Detected in Wild Birds in Australia
item.description: The deadly H5 avian influenza has been discovered in a fourth wild bird in Australia, with a fifth case suspected.

item.title: BYD’s Wang Holds Talks in Shaanxi as Visits Span Five Provinces
item.description: BYD Co. Chairman Wang Chuanfu met Shaanxi Provincial Party Secretary Zhao Yide and Governor Zhao Gang on Friday, the latest in a series of meetings with leaders across at least five provinces since March.

item.title: China’s Industrial Profit Gains Dip in Sign of Economic Weakness
item.description: The increase in China’s industrial profits softened for the first time since November, suggesting that strong exports and price gains failed to offset the drag from tepid domestic demand.

item.title: Aid Groups Flock to Venezuela In Search-and-Rescue Frenzy
item.description: Two of the US military ships used in a blockade meant to pressure Nicolás Maduro have headed back toward Venezuela, this time with rescue teams, equipment and medical aid after devastating twin earthquakes struck the country this week.

item.title: Philippine Government Plans to Raise Budget by 6% Next Year
item.description: The Philippines plans to increase the budget by 6% next year to 7.2 trillion pesos ($117 billion), according to the budget department.

item.title: Bolivia Moves to Flexible Exchange-Rate System After 15 Years
item.description: Bolivia is moving to a flexible exchange-rate system to strengthen macroeconomic stability, its Finance Ministry announced Friday.

item.title: Atlanta Fed Chief Selection Delay Gives Warsh a Say
item.description: The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta has resumed its search for a new president following the arrival of Kevin Warsh, allowing the new Fed chairman to have a say in the selection of a key official who will vote on interest rates in 2027.
